_comment: Per-platform performance baselines for hooks/bench-memory.sh perf. Each entry's avg_ms is the expected average wall-clock; tolerance is the multiplier above which scripts/bench-gate.sh reports regression. Gated scenarios fail CI when exceeded; observational ones are logged. Calibrated 2026-05-07 against synthetic 500-file corpus.

_calibration.darwin: Apple Silicon, macOS 25.x — measured locally (3 runs, avg)
_calibration.linux: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 — initial estimate ~2x of darwin baseline; will tighten after the first few CI rounds populate real distribution

_tolerance_rationale: 1.5x absorbs ~30 % runner variance + buffer. darwin baselines moved to hosted-runner ceilings (PR #6: 708ms session-start; PR #8: 1302ms session-start — same code, different runner moments). The original numbers were calibrated against a local M-series Mac, which is materially faster than the GitHub-hosted macOS runner; the gate has to pass on the runner that actually runs CI. Linux runners are tighter (~30 % variance) and the original numbers there were already safe.

stop (3 WAL passes + evidence)._skip_reason: min/max range observed at >2x even on a steady-state machine — gating would flake without per-run smoothing.
